message:
fix(http): h1 listener survives per-connection spawn failure, h2 enforces GOAWAY stream boundary

H1: Http1Listener::run previously treated every spawn_connection failure
as listener-fatal, killing the accept loop. RegionAtCapacity is now
treated as connection-scoped — the listener logs the miss and continues
accepting. A comprehensive integration test verifies the listener stays
alive after capacity rejection and recovers once a slot frees.

H2: After sending GOAWAY, PUSH_PROMISE frames carrying stream IDs above
last_stream_id were silently accepted. They are now RST with
RefusedStream per RFC 9113 §6.8, preventing the peer from pushing new
resources after graceful shutdown has been signalled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
